title: Nearby Duplicate Within K
description:
Determine whether the array contains two equal values whose indices differ by at most k. Use a sliding hash window of size up to k to track the last k elements efficiently.

    

Constraints:
    - 1 ≤ len(nums) ≤ 10^5
- -10^9 ≤ nums[i] ≤ 10^9
- 0 ≤ k ≤ 10^5
explanation:
Intuition
A set representing the last k elements seen allows O(1) checks for membership. As we extend the window by one element, we add the new value and remove the element that falls out of range, keeping the set size at most k.

Approach
1. Initial Setup: Maintain a set window and a left pointer.
2. Main Algorithm: For each right index, check if nums[right] is in the set; if yes return True. Insert it, then if window size exceeds k, remove nums[left] and advance left.
3. Return Result: Return False if no duplication within k is found.

Complexity Analysis
- Time Complexity: O(n) because the algorithm scans elements a constant
  number of times and performs O(1) work for each update or lookup.
- Space Complexity: O(k) due to the auxiliary structures required to
  store counts, prefix sums, or membership sets while ensuring fast access.

Key Insights
- Sliding windows bound memory while scanning once
- Sets provide O(1) containment checks

Common Pitfalls
- Forgetting to remove the left element breaks the size guarantee
- Using a list incurs O(k) checks per step
hints: Check membership before inserting the new value
Remove the outgoing element as the window grows
